properties.name: compareItems
properties.description:
Provide a custom function that is used to determine the ordering of
options. The compare function should return a number whose sign indicates
the relative order of the two elements: negative if a is less than b,
positive if a is greater than b, and zero if they are equal. See 'sortItems'
for more control.

(itemA: string, itemB: string, { locale }: { locale: string }) => number

properties.name: sortItems
properties.description:
Provide a method that sorts all options in the control. Overriding this
prop means that you also have to handle the sort logic for selected versus
un-selected items. If you just want to control ordering, consider the
`compareItems` prop instead.

sortItems :
(menuItems: NodeList, {
  values: string[],
  compareItems: (itemA: string, itemB: string, { locale }: { locale: string }) => number,
  locale: string,
}) => Node[]

options. The compare function should return a number whose sign indicates
the relative order of the two elements: negative if a is less than b,
positive if a is greater than b, and zero if they are equal. See 'sortItems'
for more control.

(itemA: string, itemB: string, { locale }: { locale: string }) => number

properties.name: sortItems
properties.description:

prop means that you also have to handle the sort logic for selected versus
un-selected items. If you just want to control ordering, consider the
`compareItems` prop instead.

sortItems :
(menuItems: NodeList, {
  values: string[],
  compareItems: (itemA: string, itemB: string, { locale }: { locale: string }) => number,
  locale: string,
}) => Node[]

components.description:
The Stack component is a useful layout utility in a component-based model.
This allows components to not use margin and instead delegate the
responsibility of positioning and layout to parent components.

In the case of the Stack component, it uses the spacing scale from the
Design Language in order to determine how much space there should be between
items rendered by the Stack component. It also supports a custom `gap` prop
which will allow a user to provide a custom value for the gap of the layout.

This component supports both horizontal and vertical orientations.

attributes.description:
Provide either a custom value or a step from the spacing scale to be used
as the gap in the layout

attributes.name: use-custom-gap-value
attributes.description: Turn on when passing in custom value to 'gap' attribute (ie. gap="2rem")
